old_claim.note: Theory of Evolution (Darwin) provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No direct edge remains from psychology to Darwinian evolution because the Psychology node is currently dated to the 1760s, while the Darwinian natural-selection node is source-anchored to 1859.

source_shape.source_locator: Library of Congress record for Darwin's On the origin of species by means of natural selection, published in London by J. Murray in 1859.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source anchors Darwinian natural selection to an 1859 publication, after the current Psychology node date.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: A node dated to the 1760s cannot directly depend on an 1859 Darwinian theory node without either changing psychology's scope or creating a temporal contradiction.

ontology_before: The graph modeled broad psychology as directly downstream of Darwinian natural selection despite psychology being dated nearly a century earlier.
ontology_after: Psychology remains downstream of experimental psychology and printing infrastructure, while Darwinian natural selection is preserved as a later biology theory node.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: psychology must not directly depend on theory_of_evolution_darwin under the current 1760s psychology scope.
would_reject_if: The Psychology node is rescoped and redated to post-1859 evolutionary psychology or comparative psychology.
A separate later psychology node is introduced that intentionally depends on Darwinian natural selection.
A source shows the current Psychology node date is wrong and should move after 1859 with downstream rewiring.
short_reason: The current psychology node predates the source-backed Darwinian evolution node.
